{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# The Transformer Hardware Lottery {#sec-hardware-lottery}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- Why *this* architecture won: not just that it models language well, but
     that it maps beautifully onto the hardware we happen to build. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]

## The hardware lottery, stated

<!-- Hooker's thesis [@hooker2021hardware]: ideas win partly by fitting the
     available hardware. Frame the transformer as a lottery winner. -->

## GPUs think in matmuls

<!-- The transformer is (almost) all dense matmuls; how that saturates
     tensor cores where recurrence does not. -->

## The memory wall

<!-- Arithmetic intensity, roofline; why bandwidth, not FLOPs, is often the
     bottleneck. Attention's quadratic memory traffic. -->

## FlashAttention as a case study

<!-- Tiling + recomputation to keep attention in fast memory; the same math,
     a hardware-shaped implementation. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO: roofline estimate for a given model/GPU. -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
